title: Incident Channel Mix Trending
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-180d@d
| eval ch=lower(coalesce(contact_type, channel, u_channel, "unknown"))
| eval _time=coalesce(opened_at, _time)
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count by _time, ch
| eventstats sum(count) as week_total by _time
| eval share_pct=round(100*count/nullif(week_total,0),1)
| sort _time ch
description: Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service ROI; trending aligns workforce management with ITIL service desk strategy and digital adoption goals.
value: Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service ROI; trending aligns workforce management with ITIL service desk strategy and digital adoption goals.
implementation: (1) Normalize `ch` with a lookup for legacy values; (2) Exclude automated monitoring tickets via `caller_id` or `category` filters; (3) Review rising phone share weekly with service owners when portal adoption programs are active.
visualization: Stacked area chart (channel share % over weeks), Line chart (portal % trend), Table (raw counts by channel).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`contact_type`, `channel`, `u_channel`, `opened_at`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Normalize `ch` with a lookup for legacy values; (2) Exclude automated monitoring tickets via `caller_id` or `category` filters; (3) Review rising phone share weekly with service owners when portal adoption programs are active.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-180d@d
| eval ch=lower(coalesce(contact_type, channel, u_channel, "unknown"))
| eval _time=coalesce(opened_at, _time)
| bin _time span=1w
| stats count by _time, ch
| eventstats sum(count) as week_total by _time
| eval share_pct=round(100*count/nullif(week_total,0),1)
| sort _time ch
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Channel Mix Trending** ‚Äî Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service ROI; trending aligns workforce management with ITIL service desk strategy and digital adoption goals.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`contact_type`, `channel`, `u_channel`, `opened_at`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **ch** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **_time** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, ch** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **share_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked area chart (channel share % over weeks), Line chart (portal % trend), Table (raw counts by channel).
grandmaExplanation: Channel mix (portal, phone, chat, email) influences staffing models and self-service return on investment ‚Äî so tickets, changes, and incidents are on track and nothing is silently missed.
